thesis: Meta owns the best P&L in the Mag7 (+33% revenue, 82% gross margin, ~33% net) yet trades at one of the lowest forward multiples (17.2x FY26) after a ~29% de-rating driven by $125-145B AI capex, near-zero FCF-after-capex, and a June equity-raise scare it has since called 'pure speculation.' Valuation clusters at $700-830 (base DCF, 5Y mean reversion, consensus PT) if the funding question resolves benignly, and Wells Fargo's new compute-resale thesis (+16% to 2027 EPS) offers a fresh non-ad monetization path. It is the Mag7's highest-variance bet: base $629 (+12%), bull $845 (+50%), bear $386 (-31%), with the July 27 addiction trial and July 29 Q2 earnings as the near-term direction-setters.

Microsoft is the best-positioned AI hyperscaler yet the worst-performing Magnificent Seven stock of 2026, funding its ~$190B capex 100% from operating cash flow with no equity raise while Azure still grows 40% and margins lead the group. At 19.3x forward EPS near a three-year-low multiple with the stock washed out and bouncing off the $345-352 support zone, the drawdown is a macro/valuation reset rather than a fundamental one, with fresh bulge-bracket targets rising and Michael Burry's Dec-2028 $700 LEAPs marking a contrarian bottom bet. Base case ~$465 (+25%); the late-July FY-end print and FY27 capex guide are the swing factors.

Bilibili is a profitable-transition China media name trading near its 52-week low at 12.7x forward earnings with ~20% of its market cap in net cash, where the market pays almost nothing for a stacked 2H26 game pipeline (NCard, San Wang, Lumi Master). A fresh $300M buyback and a Morgan Stanley Overweight/$31 'Research Tactical Idea' (June 24) aim to offset the Tencent-stake sell-off overhang that is the key single-name pressure. Fundamentally intact and catalyst-supported but still overhang-hostage: base case $21.50 (+26%), with July's NCard launch as the validation gate.

Broadcom is the highest-margin, most-contracted way to own the AI accelerator buildout, and its fundamentals kept improving in June — capped by the June 24 OpenAI 'Jalapeño' custom inference ASIC win — even as the stock slipped to $377.75 on the Nasdaq correction, a hawkish-Fed dot plot, and the Hormuz oil shock. At 19.5x forward (a ~38% discount to AI-design peers) with named multi-year XPU customers, a renewed 5-year Google TPU pact, and ~$30B of AI bookings, the pullback looks macro/positioning-driven rather than fundamental. Accumulate on weakness: 12-month base $465 (+23%), bull $582 (+54%) if the FY27 >$100B AI framework is raised; key catalyst is the ~Sep 3 Q3 print.

GM is the cheapest highly-profitable automaker at ~5.5x forward earnings with the legacy cohort's best operating margin (9.4%), ~$10B automotive FCF, and a $6B buyback retiring ~8% of the float. The chart has rolled over (lost its 50-day, bearish MACD, testing the SMA200 near $75) as the Fed turns more hawkish and ~$50M of late-May C-suite selling at $80-85 now sits above spot, but DCF base, consensus PT, and base-case P/E all cluster near $92 (~19% upside). Buy-on-weakness in the $74-77 zone into the July 21 Q2 print; bull case $135 needs the beat-and-raise cycle, shrinking EV losses, and the new automation/robotics optionality to deliver.

Micron's June 24 FQ3'26 print was the steepest earnings inflection in semiconductor history — $41.46B revenue, $25.11 EPS (+24% beat), 84.9% gross margin, and an FQ4 guide of ~$50B that blew past the ~$43.6B estimate, with HBM3E/HBM4 contractually booked through 2027 and demand into 2028. At 7.7x FY+1 earnings (15.7x on current-FY EPS) with $51B operating cash flow and self-funded US capacity while peers dilute, the memory supercycle re-rate is intact, flipping our probability-weighted fair value (~$1,250) modestly above the $1,154 spot. Bullish but no longer a chase: the marquee catalyst has cleared, the stock is stretched near an all-time high, and a fresh June 25 DRAM price-fixing class action targets the exact margin story — accumulate on a pullback toward $1,000-1,050, don't pay the high.

A fallen-angel value setup: once a 30x compounder, LULU now trades at ~9x trailing / 10x forward earnings and 1.16x sales after a 53% one-year collapse, with a 55.7% gross margin, 32% ROE, no net debt, and insiders buying on the way down (Director Charles Bergh ~$1.5M). The bear case is real and structural — Americas comps -5% to -6%, share loss to Alo/Vuori/Skims, ~$240-320M/yr tariff drag, and a cut FY26 EPS guide (~$11) that may make 9-10x fair rather than cheap. Speculative value buy: base $132 (+16%) on stabilization, bull $180 on the Heidi O'Neill turnaround, $88 bear tail if US comps keep eroding or China cracks; Sept 3 print is the swing factor.

NVIDIA at $200 trades at just 15.7x FY28 earnings (22.3x current-year) — the cheapest forward multiple in its modern history and the cheapest of any AI-compute leader — despite 85% revenue growth, 63% net margins and a fortress ~$40B net-cash balance sheet. Micron's June 24 blowout hard-confirmed the HBM/Vera-Rubin demand leg while NVDA lagged the semis melt-up, leaving asymmetric 12-month upside (base $281, +40%). The call is Bullish-not-euphoric: China data-center-GPU sales remain banned at baseline (the H200 thaw is partial and reversible) and the AI capex cycle leans on credit the Fed is no longer easing into.

Alphabet is the AI capital cycle's marquee test case with the only full stack (TPU silicon, Gemini, Cloud +63%, 2B-user Search) and the best fundamental momentum in megacap, but a fresh DeepMind talent exodus (Shazeer to OpenAI; Jumper, Adler, Pritzel to Anthropic) plus an $84.75B dilutive raise and FY26 FCF set to fall ~72% under $180-190B capex have pulled the stock back to roughly fair value at 24.3x forward. At $353 it round-trips to its 5Y-average multiple with balanced risk/reward: base case ~$378 (+7%), bull ~$502 on TPU externalization and Gemini shipping, bear ~$235 on an adverse antitrust + capex-without-payoff + talent-drain scenario. The defining overhang is moderate policy risk from active DOJ search-antitrust remedies (DC Circuit appeal) plus the AdTech/AdX case.

NIO's turnaround is real — Q1 2026 revenue +112% YoY, gross margin at 19% (from 7.6%), ~18.8% vehicle margin, and record 1H26 deliveries of 191,123 (+67.4%) with June a 2026 high — yet the July 1 print handed skeptics a first crack, as Q2's 107,658 vehicles fell just short of the 110-115K guide. At a currency-consistent ~0.9x sales the stock still prices years of misses; the bet is that Q2 margins held ~18% and ADR flows stabilize against a hawkish-Fed, price-war, cost-inflation backdrop. NEUTRAL with a long bias, base case $7.00 (+38%).

TSM is the sole-source toll booth on the AI buildout — every NVDA GPU, AVGO/Google/Amazon ASIC and AMD accelerator routes through its fabs, and it is raising 3nm quotes ~15% in H2 2026 with record 66% gross margins and >30% FY26 growth. After a +116% run off the 52-week low to fresh highs at $477.57 (23.7x forward, ~98th-percentile trailing P/E, above customers NVDA ~17x and AVGO ~21x), the stock is fairly valued on its own history — Street PTs are now rising (BofA $590, Susquehanna $575) but the price sits at the $488 mean, leaving a momentum/quality hold rather than a value entry. The dominant risks are the unhedgeable Taiwan geopolitical tail, US tariff/export policy, and a hawkish Warsh Fed against hot 4.1% PCE compressing a rich multiple.

Costco remains the best-run business in retail — Q3 FY26 posted +11.6% revenue, +15% EPS, digital +21%, and a renewal rate that rose to 92.2% a year after a fee hike, all underwritten by a ~$5.4B near-100%-margin membership annuity and a 29% ROE. Since May's $1,096 record the stock has quietly de-rated ~15% to $935 — a shade below its 5Y-average ~42x forward multiple — even as it lagged a record-high, best-quarter-in-six-years market that structurally disfavors a ~0.87-beta staple. The debate is unchanged and purely valuation: ~41x forward against a 4.45% 10Y and a Fed debating a hike; accumulate on weakness ($900-920) with a base case ~$1,030, expecting outperformance only if the tape rotates back to defensives.

Li Auto has become the worst EV-trio performer, sliding to a fresh 52-week low of $11.74 after Q1 vehicle margin collapsed to ~6% and June deliveries fell 14.8% YoY, confirming its EREV-to-BEV transition is destroying mix faster than it rebuilds it. A ~$10.5B net-cash cushion (roughly 90% of market cap) and a refreshed lineup (L8/L6/i9) plus a guided ~10% Q2 gross-margin recovery form a falsifiable trough thesis, but the Street is cutting targets and spot has fallen through the Street-low PT. Verdict: hold — the asymmetry is favorable on paper, but buying ahead of the late-August margin print with volume still falling and China-ADR flows draining is paying for a promise not yet kept.

Tiger Brokers is mid-way through a two-year CSRC-mandated wind-down of its unlicensed mainland-China cross-border brokerage (sell-only since June 12), a regulatory story that dominates and keeps policy risk severe. Ex-penalty profitability is intact (~$30M+/qtr) and the ~90% non-mainland franchise still compounds at +28% YoY, with the stock at ~0.93x book, a $50M buyback live, and Avenir/Li Lin lifting its stake to 10.9% near the lows. But the sub-5x forward multiple rests on a pre-crackdown EPS the Street is still cutting; base case ~$5.50 on normalized earnings with a real $3 bear tail — size for the binary, don't chase the headline upside.

Alibaba is a hyper-growth AI/cloud platform (Qwen, +38% cloud) bolted onto a barely-growing, subsidy-drained e-commerce business now in its first FCF-negative year. At $95.98 and 10.6x forward it screens cheap and sits on net cash, but the June collapse stacked two discrete shocks on the macro fade: the June 8 Pentagon 1260H 'Chinese Military Company' designation (Alibaba sued the DoD June 23) and Anthropic's June 24 accusation that Alibaba distilled Claude to train Qwen. Deeply oversold (RSI ~5, fresh 52W low) and fundamentally interesting, but patience over urgency: base case ~$120 (+25%), bull $180 if cloud, a 1260H court win, and the IP dispute all resolve; $72 bear tail on sanctions/IP escalation.

A pre-revenue quantum-photonics story trading at ~505x sales on just $4.3M of mostly-acquired revenue, held up only by a ~$986M cash pile (funded by 64% share dilution in a year) and pure quantum-theme momentum. Tellingly, the stock fell ~19% in June even as President Trump's June 22 quantum executive orders and blowout peer prints lifted the sector, because concrete federal validation went to Rigetti/D-Wave/IONQ, not QUBT — which also carries a short-seller (Capybara, Iceberg) and fraud-litigation overhang. Highly speculative, venture-style total-loss-tolerant sizing only; base ~$10, bull $22, bear $4.50 (net cash).

Apple remains the defensive Mag7 name in a capital-intensity-averse regime — $101B FCF, zero dilution, a working (if Gemini-rented) Siri — but the price already discounts a good outcome at 30x forward. The new, unambiguously negative catalyst is the memory supercycle: DRAM/NAND cost inflation forced June 25 MacBook/iPad price hikes of $100–300, the stock's worst day since April 2025, and threatens the 49% gross-margin high into a shrinking smartphone unit market. Hold as ballast, don't add at 30x; better entry at the $270–289 moving-average shelf.

QCOM delivered a substantive June 24 Investor Day — naming Meta as its first data-center CPU customer and roughly doubling its FY29 non-handset target to $40B (data center >$15B) — then sold the news, falling 18% to $184.79 as the market balked at a flagship Dragonfly roadmap that doesn't ship in volume until 2028. The drop restored the margin of safety: at 16.9x forward the analyst mean PT ($215) now sits ~17% ABOVE the price (inverse of the pre-event value-trap signal) after a coordinated wave of sell-side target hikes. Accumulate on weakness — base $205 (+11%), bull $260 (+41%) if the Meta deal proves repeatable and the AI200 ships on time — while sizing for 2028 execution risk plus the Apple modem/license cliff and ~46% China/Huawei exposure.

Seagate is a genuinely transformed AI-datacenter storage oligopolist — 44% revenue growth, 36% operating margins, HAMR/Mozaic cost leadership, and nearline capacity sold out through CY27 at rising prices — but at $965 (35.5x forward / 91.7x trailing, >99th percentile of its own history) the stock trades at/above the average analyst target of $942 and has already paid for much of the supercycle. The June 26 −12% SK Hynix memory rout, a fresh bearish MACD cross, and broad C-suite selling into the spike flag momentum/cyclical-top risk. Base- and conservative-case fair values ($679–$830) sit below spot; only the optimistic $25–30/TB pricing path justifies the price — Neutral, trim-into-strength, WDC is the cheaper way to own the identical thesis.

Lemonade has inflected from cash-burn to a credible path-to-profitability story — +71% revenue, gross profit +159%, net loss ratio down to 63%, IFP $1.33B (+32%, ten straight quarters of accelerating growth), a Tesla FSD auto partnership scaling car insurance +60% YoY, and four consecutive quarters of positive adjusted FCF, all self-funded with net cash toward guided Q4 2026 EBITDA breakeven and FY2027 profitability. But after a +12% June rally on the small-cap rotation (+50% YTD, RSI 73, above both moving averages), the stock at $65 trades above the $62 consensus PT at ~5.9x sales — the priciest name in its insurtech cohort — leaving a thin margin of safety and a binary, execution-gated setup. Fairly-to-fully valued Hold; prefer adding on a profitability beat or a pullback into the $50-58 zone.

WeRide is the broadest international L4 robotaxi franchise (~2,800 AVs, 40+ cities, permits in 8 markets, Uber launches in Madrid and Zurich), trading at fresh all-time lows near $5.82 with ~46% of market cap in cash and ~4 years of runway. But it is a pre-profit China ADR burning >$200M/yr with no breakeven guidance into a hike-biased Fed and a persistent delisting overhang, and Nvidia just exited its entire stake. Hold/speculative-accumulate in small size near the cash-supported lows; base case ~$9.00 (+55%), bear $4.20 (-28%), bull $13.50 (+132%).

DRAM is the first pure-play memory ETF, a concentrated actively-managed basket of the HBM/DRAM oligopoly (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron) plus NAND/HDD names, expressing the AI-driven memory supercycle in which >50% of DRAM wafer capacity has shifted to HBM and shortages are guided to persist beyond 2026. It is the cleanest listed vehicle for the thesis that AI inference is now memory-bound, up ~166% since its April 2026 launch. The trade-off is extreme cyclicality and concentration: the same pure-memory exposure that amplifies the upcycle would amplify any pricing rollover when new HBM fabs ramp in 2026-2027.

Tesla is the market's purest narrative-vs-numbers trade: a late-June risk-on rally lifted it to $420.60 (~168x forward EPS, above its own 5-year P/E ceiling) even as a hawkish Fed and rotation out of high-multiple tech pressure exactly this long-duration, no-earnings-floor profile. The story is advancing (robotaxi ramp, ~1.28M FSD subs, paid ride-hailing going live) but numbers stay weakest in the group at a 4.2% operating margin and ~301x FCF, and no cash-flow model reaches $420 (bull DCF ~$287). Hold/trim into strength: base $420 (fairly valued), bear $212 on a de-rating or Q2 delivery miss, bull $717 only on a delivery beat plus driverless-at-scale proof; size it as a venture sleeve, not the core, and respect the binary July 2 delivery print.

Pony AI is executing operationally (Q1'26 revenue +145% YoY, robotaxi +395%, only China player with fully-driverless commercial ops in all four Tier-1 cities, ~$1.07B cash vs $16M debt), but the stock has fallen to fresh 52-week lows at $6.95 (-72% from high) on a triple hit: a hawkish higher-for-longer Fed, the persistent China-ADR delisting/data-security overhang, and China's April 29 nationwide freeze on new AV permits after the Baidu Apollo Go Wuhan mass-malfunction. At ~13x EV/S it remains the richest multiple in the AV cohort and years from profit; net cash of ~$2.98/share is a hard floor. Bearish/Hold (composite 42): base $12 (+73%), bear $5 (-28%), bull $20 (+188%) — the permit-freeze lift, the ~Aug 18 Q2 print, and the Nov founder lockup decide the outcome.

XPeng is the China-EV trio's highest-variance bet, trading near a fresh 52-week low ($13.24) after a ~20% monthly drop as a hawkish Fed and a China-ADR decoupling punished its long-duration 'physical AI' optionality; Q1 revenue fell 17.6% YoY into a renewed loss and the 23.1x forward P/E is the richest of the group. But the just-confirmed Q2 delivery snap-back (103,295 units, meeting guidance, June +15.9% YoY) plus a record ~20% gross margin, a net-cash balance sheet, and VW VLA-2.0 licensing offer concrete offsets. Bearish-to-Neutral: base case $16 (+21%) is attractive but the live $7.50 bear case only clears once the volume rebound converts to revenue/margin at the mid-August print.

Corning has re-rated ~5x (+188% in 6 months) from 'legacy glass maker' to 'physical layer of AI infrastructure' on the optical-fiber datacenter boom, anchored by a ~$6B Meta contract and a multiyear NVIDIA partnership with up to $3.2B of strategic capital and an upgraded Springboard plan ($20B run-rate by end-2026, $35-40B by 2030). The business is genuinely better, but at 123x trailing / 60x forward P/E it is priced richer than NVIDIA for flawless multi-year execution. Every published analyst target sits below spot (mean ~$206, Street-high $228 vs $255), so risk/reward skews to the downside near-term despite the strong secular thesis.

AMD has round-tripped the early-June semi crash and printed a fresh all-time high of $580.91 (+7.7% on Cantor Fitzgerald's Street-high $700 target), leaving it at 44x FY27 / 79x FY26 earnings with the analyst mean target ($506) now 13% below spot and every triangulated central fair value (mean reversion $428, peer parity ~$375, base DCF $510) below the price. The MI450/Helios AI-accelerator ramp and OpenAI/Meta/Oracle contracts give real multi-year visibility, but the entire premium rests on a Q3 ramp not yet in reported numbers, and the quant score (38/Bearish) fades a +196% three-month momentum run into a hawkish-Fed backdrop. Verdict: Trim/Hold, accumulate on pullbacks toward $540/$450 rather than chase the breakout.

Intel has re-rated to a 52-week-high $139.63 (+278% YTD) on reported Apple and Google-TPU foundry wins plus a Trump endorsement and a ~9.9% US-government stake, but the fundamentals lag hard: ~90x forward P/E, 13x sales, negative GAAP EPS, -$8.3B FCF, and external foundry revenue of only ~$174M against a segment losing ~$2.4B/quarter. Every valuation method clusters fair value at $85-102 (roughly 30% below spot) and even the duopoly-bull case is now flat, so the stock prices a near-certain foundry-turnaround outcome on deals companies have not confirmed. Verdict: Hold, tilting to Trim into strength; wait for a $100-115 accumulation zone rather than chase the high, with the July 23 print and any Apple/Google/NVDA confirmation as the swing events.
